questions.question: Explain the three key methods used for transaction management in Android SQLite with an example.
keyPoints: endTransaction() commits if successful, rolls back otherwise
Must use try-finally pattern to ensure endTransaction() is always called

questions.question: Discuss the importance of transactions in Android SQLite database operations. Explain with a practical example how transactions improve both data integrity and performance.
keyPoints: Data integrity: atomic operations ensure all-or-nothing behavior
Performance: batch operations are 10-25x faster with transactions
Without transactions, each operation has its own implicit transaction with disk I/O
Practical example: multi-table insert or batch insert
Best practices: short transactions, background threads, check results

questions.question: How does the Room database handle transactions? Compare with raw SQLite transaction management.
keyPoints: Room uses @Transaction annotation on DAO methods
Room provides runInTransaction() for programmatic transactions

questions.question: What are the common pitfalls when using SQLite transactions in Android? How can they be avoided?
keyPoints: Not calling setTransactionSuccessful(): transaction silently rolls back
Long-running transactions with network calls: keep transactions short
Running on main thread: use background threads or coroutines
Not checking operation results: verify return values of insert/update/delete
